title: Spec: LR-FHSS mode negotiation
description:
Define LR-FHSS mode advertisement and negotiation in spec.

## Location
Add to spec/02-physical-link.md

## Content

### Mode advertisement
- DIO option: LR_FHSS_SUPPORTED flag
- Announce option: LR_FHSS_CAPABLE flag (node can TX/RX LR-FHSS)

### Negotiation
- Gateway sets LR_FHSS_SUPPORTED in DIO
- Capable nodes MAY switch to LR-FHSS for uplink
- Gateway MUST support dual-mode RX (standard + LR-FHSS)
- Downlink: gateway uses same mode as node's last uplink

### Parameters
- Data rate: DR8-DR11 (LR-FHSS modes per LoRaWAN regional params)
- Hopping: OCW (operating channel width) 137kHz or 336kHz
- Coding rate: 1/3 or 2/3

### Backward compatibility
- SX1276 nodes ignore LR_FHSS flags, continue standard LoRa
- Mixed networks work: gateway bridges both modes
notes:
## Backwards Compatibility

**No flag day required.**

- Old nodes (SX1276): Standard LoRa only, ignore LR_FHSS flags
- New nodes (SX1262): Can use LR-FHSS when gateway supports it
- Mixed network: Gateway MUST support dual-mode RX (standard + LR-FHSS)

**Requirements:**
- LR_FHSS_SUPPORTED flag in DIO MUST use reserved bits
- Nodes without SX1262 MUST ignore the flag
- Gateway MUST receive both standard LoRa and LR-FHSS on same channel
- Node-to-node: both use standard LoRa (common ground) unless both are SX1262 and have negotiated LR-FHSS
- Downlink: gateway uses same mode as node's last uplink

**Negotiation:**
- Announce includes LR_FHSS_CAPABLE flag (1 bit in app_data)
- Two LR-FHSS-capable nodes MAY use LR-FHSS for direct P2P
- Fallback: standard LoRa always works
close_reason: Spec section 3.6 added to 02-physical-link.md with negotiation, parameters, compatibility details from notes
